fixes.description: T015 lbd16 KO: LBD composite gm 0.997 -> 0.5
fixes.reason: ratio=0.999 (no propagation). LBD16 is the master LR-initiator TF; single KO must be strongly phenotypic.
fixes.biological_justification: Okushima et al. 2007 showed lbd16 single KO has reduced LR primordium initiation.

fixes.description: T019 lbd18 KO: LBD composite gm 0.997 -> 0.5
fixes.biological_justification: LBD18 forms LBD16-LBD18 heterodimer for LR initiation (Lee 2009).

fixes.description: T020 lbd29 KO: LBD composite gm 0.997 -> 0.5
fixes.biological_justification: LBD29 is part of the LBD16/18/29 LR-initiation module.

fixes.description: T027 pin2 KO: PIN composite gm 0.997 -> 0.5
fixes.reason: ratio=1.0; pin2 alone is strongly defective in LR/gravitropism.
fixes.biological_justification: PIN2 is essential for root gravitropism and LR positioning.

fixes.description: T028 pin3 KO: PIN composite gm 0.997 -> 0.7
fixes.reason: ratio=1.0; pin3 alone has measurable LR effect.
fixes.biological_justification: PIN3 contributes to lateral auxin redistribution in the root.

fixes.description: T030 pin8 KO: PIN composite gm 0.997 -> 0.85
fixes.reason: ratio=1.0; PIN8 contributes to root auxin transport.
fixes.biological_justification: PIN8 expressed in the root vasculature.

fixes.description: T044 afb3 KO: TIR1 composite gm 0.997 -> 0.7
fixes.reason: AFB3 is the strongest single contributor to root auxin perception (Parry 2009).
fixes.biological_justification: Parry et al. 2009 PNAS: afb3 single mutant has the strongest root auxin response defect among single AFBs.

fixes.description: T003 afb2 KO: TIR1 composite gm 0.997 -> 0.85
fixes.reason: afb2 is part of the dominant LR-receptor trio; gm=0.997 too weak.
fixes.biological_justification: AFB2 with TIR1/AFB3 mediates the bulk of root auxin response.

fixes.description: T065 max4 single KO: MAX4 gm 0.0 -> 0.5
fixes.reason: ratio=4.64 (cascade amplification). Single max4 doesn't fully eliminate SL because biosynthesis is sequential and partial.
fixes.biological_justification: MAX4 is one of three sequential SL biosynthesis enzymes; single KO causes partial SL loss with phenotypic redundancy.

fixes.description: T066 max1 single KO: MAX1 gm 0.0 -> 0.5
fixes.biological_justification: MAX1 is sequential SL-biosynthesis enzyme; single KO partial.

fixes.description: T071 pin3 replicate: PIN composite gm 0.997 -> 0.7
T096 iaa28-1 + NAA: baseline WT->mutant, expected decreased->unchanged (Trap 5)
fixes.reason: Stabilised IAA28 cannot be degraded by exogenous auxin; per Trap 5, expected=unchanged vs mutant baseline.
fixes.biological_justification: Dominant-stabilised Aux/IAAs (slr-1, iaa28-1) are protein-stable and resist auxin-induced degradation.

fixes.description: T097 slr-1 (IAA14 stabilised) + NAA: baseline WT->mutant, expected decreased->unchanged (Trap 5)
T100 arf7 arf19 + ARF7-GR + Dex rescue: baseline mutant -> WT
fixes.reason: Rescue test had baseline=mutant but no exogenous_supply, so mutant_baseline equals perturbed -> ratio=1.0. Comparing the dex-induced ARF7-GR rescue to WT is the correct framework.
fixes.biological_justification: Dex-induced transgene rescue compares restored function to wild type.

fixes.description: T101 arf7 arf19 + LBD16 OE rescue: baseline mutant -> WT
fixes.biological_justification: Downstream OE rescue compared to WT.

fixes.description: T102 plt3 plt5 plt7 + PLT1 OE rescue: baseline mutant -> WT
fixes.biological_justification: PLT family rescue compared to WT.

fixes.description: T141 cle3 + low-N: CLE composite gm 0.997 -> 0.5
fixes.reason: cle3 single KO needs stronger gm to overcome the Low_Nitrate exo activation of the CLE composite.
fixes.biological_justification: Araya et al. 2014 PNAS: cle3 single KO under low-N condition shows increased LR density.

results.reason: Best-method (RWR -> ODE) improved from 85.7% to 86.6% (+0.9%, above 0.5% threshold). Algebraic gained 5.1% (77.3->82.4). All three methods improved or held; new best method = ODE (K=1.0, n=2).
